title: Sick-day Silky Egyptian Flax Eggs Confit
description: A silky, comforting Egyptian-inspired dish perfect for a sick day, made with flax eggs and dairy-free-friendly confit tomato. Serve with a side of toasted bread for dipping in the rich sauce.

ingredients.name: Flaxseed
ingredients.notes: Ground flaxseed used to make flax eggs.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Used to mix with ground flaxseed to make flax eggs.

ingredients.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Used for cooking the confit tomato.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped onion adds flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds depth to the sauce.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a smoky flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Fresh Parsley
ingredients.notes: Chopped parsley adds freshness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Used to brighten the flavors in the sauce.

ingredients.name: Pita Bread
ingredients.notes: Toasted pita bread is used to soak up the rich sauce.

ingredients.name: Nutritional Yeast
ingredients.notes: Nutritional yeast adds a cheesy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Chopped Fresh Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Chopped cilantro adds freshness to the dish.

instructions: Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the confit tomato, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, mix the ground flaxseed and water in a small bowl to make the flax eggs. Let it sit for 5 minutes to thicken.
Add the flax eggs to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add the chopped parsley, lemon juice, nutritional yeast, and chopped cilantro. Stir to combine.
Serve the confit tomato mixture over toasted pita bread and enjoy!
